summary: Task 06 is a presentation-layer wiring change: one keybinding entry, one view-switch handler, one filter-guard widening, one dialog-guard restructure, one help-text line. No user-controlled input flows into SQL, shell, or filesystem paths. The /proc/self/io probe uses a static path with filepath.Clean inside stat.CheckIOAvailable (audited in Task 01). The local-mode guard correctly fails closed on remote connections. OWASP Top 10: no new vectors introduced.

owaspCoverage.A01_BrokenAccessControl: pass — db.Local guard blocks the screen on remote connections before any IO probe runs; the cancel/terminate/mask dialogs still require view.Name == 'activity', so widening dialogChangeAge to include procpidstat does NOT grant terminate/cancel access on the new view
owaspCoverage.A03_Injection: n/a — no new query template variables; QueryAgeThresh sanitization on dialogChangeAge is owned by changeQueryAge() which already validates input via parseHumanTimeString; widening the dialog entry-point does not bypass validation
owaspCoverage.A04_InsecureDesign: pass — handler implements defense in depth: db.Local check first (cheap, no syscall), then IO probe (one syscall, error informational only). Static map mutation is deliberately scoped: the runtime CollectExtra/IOAvailable patches are applied to a local value copy then assigned to config.view, leaving the static views map unaffected until viewSwitchHandler is invoked elsewhere
owaspCoverage.A05_SecurityMisconfiguration: pass — printCmdline messages are static strings, no format-string injection (the %s wrapper is used where needed); no debug logging added
owaspCoverage.A09_LoggingMonitoring: n/a — only cmdline UI messages, no log sinks

hardcodedSecrets: none — no credentials, keys, tokens, or connection strings in the diff

additionalNotes.guardCorrectness: Verified the original (d > dialogFilter && d <= dialogChangeAge) compound covered exactly {dialogCancelQuery=3, dialogTerminateBackend=4, dialogCancelGroup=5, dialogTerminateGroup=6, dialogSetMask=7, dialogChangeAge=8} from the iota declaration in dialog.go. The new explicit enumeration in the first guard preserves the activity-only constraint for the five destructive dialogs. The second guard isolates dialogChangeAge with the allowlist {activity, procpidstat}. This split cannot accidentally grant termination capability on procpidstat — failure mode is fail-closed.
additionalNotes.ioProbeFailureMode: stat.CheckIOAvailable returns the OS error verbatim; the handler discards the error after the boolean check (ioErr == nil). Permission-denied is the expected and only meaningful failure; no error wrapping needed because the warning string is fixed by the tech-spec to a deterministic user-facing message. No information leak (error text is not printed).
